7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L2b",null,{"children":["$","$2c",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@2d"}]}]
9:["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most people invest significant money in their laptop or desktop and then connect it to a monitor they bought on impulse or inherited from an old setup. This is backwards."}}]
a:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"You look at your monitor for eight hours a day. The quality of that experience — sharpness, colour accuracy, size, whether it causes eye strain — affects your work quality and energy levels in ways that are consistent and cumulative."}}]
b:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This guide covers what actually matters in a home office monitor and the best options across four price tiers."}}]
c:["$","h2","4",{"id":"what-specs-actually-matter","children":"What Specs Actually Matter"}]
d:["$","h3","5",{"id":"resolution","children":"Resolution"}]
e:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For home office use, the minimum useful resolution is <strong>1440p (2560×1440)</strong>. At 27 inches, 1080p is visibly pixelated for text-heavy work. 1440p at 27 inches hits the sweet spot of sharpness and GPU requirement."}}]
f:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"4K (3840×2160) becomes meaningful at 32 inches and above. Below that, the sharpness improvement is marginal and requires scaling that can cause interface issues in some applications."}}]
10:["$","h3","8",{"id":"panel-type","children":"Panel Type"}]
11:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>IPS</strong>: Wide colour gamut, accurate colours, excellent viewing angles. The right choice for most home office users."}}]
12:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>OLED</strong>: The best contrast ratio and colour accuracy available. Can suffer from burn-in with static interface elements — less suitable for pure office use."}}]
13:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>VA</strong>: Better contrast than IPS, worse viewing angles. Reasonable if you sit directly in front of the screen."}}]
14:["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>TN</strong>: Fast response times, poor colours and viewing angles. Not recommended for office work."}}]
15:["$","h3","13",{"id":"refresh-rate","children":"Refresh Rate"}]
16:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For office work, <strong>60Hz is entirely sufficient</strong>. 120Hz makes scrolling smoother and can reduce eye strain for some users, but it is not a meaningful productivity factor. Do not pay a premium for high refresh rates on a home office monitor."}}]
17:["$","h3","15",{"id":"usb-c-with-power-delivery","children":"USB-C with Power Delivery"}]
18:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single USB-C cable that carries video signal and charges your laptop simultaneously is the most practical feature a home office monitor can have. It eliminates a cable, keeps the desk clean, and simplifies the workflow of plugging in a laptop. Look for at least <strong>65W power delivery</strong> for adequate laptop charging."}}]
19:["$","h2","17",{"id":"best-monitors-for-home-office-2026","children":"Best Monitors for Home Office 2026"}]
1a:["$","h3","18",{"id":"best-budget-dell-p2422h-24-inch-1080p-180","children":"Best Budget: Dell P2422H (24-inch 1080p) — ~£180"}]
1b:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The P2422H is the workhorse of office environments for good reason: accurate colours, excellent build quality, a great ergonomic stand with height, tilt, pivot, and swivel adjustment, and four USB-A ports. Adequate for document and browser work."}}]
1c:["$","h3","20",{"id":"best-mid-range-lg-27un880-27-inch-4k-450","children":"Best Mid-Range: LG 27UN880 (27-inch 4K) — ~£450"}]
1d:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The best mid-range monitor for home office use. 4K IPS panel, USB-C 96W power delivery, and an articulating Ergo stand that eliminates the need for a separate monitor arm. One cable between laptop and monitor — this alone justifies the price step up."}}]
1e:["$","h3","22",{"id":"best-ultrawide-dell-ultrasharp-u3423we-34-inch-ultrawide-650","children":"Best Ultrawide: Dell UltraSharp U3423WE (34-inch Ultrawide) — ~£650"}]
1f:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The ultrawide of choice for multi-tasking. IPS Black panel delivers contrast closer to VA without the viewing angle trade-off. Built-in KVM switch allows one monitor to control two computers. USB-C 90W charging. The best monitor for developers and analysts."}}]
20:["$","h3","24",{"id":"best-premium-lg-ultrafine-32uq85r-32-inch-4k-700","children":"Best Premium: LG UltraFine 32UQ85R (32-inch 4K) — ~£700"}]
21:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Excellent colour accuracy, USB-C 96W, and a good stand with height and pivot adjustment. The right choice if you do any creative work alongside office tasks."}}]
22:["$","h2","26",{"id":"single-vs-dual-monitor","children":"Single vs. Dual Monitor"}]
23:["$","p","27",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single large monitor (32–34 inches) is often more productive than two smaller ones. The visual break between two screens creates a mental context-switching cost. A single 34-inch ultrawide gives comparable screen real estate with a continuous workspace."}}]
24:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Dual monitors make sense when you genuinely reference content on one screen while creating on another: code with documentation, video editing with media browser, financial modelling with source data."}}]
25:["$","h2","29",{"id":"the-stand-question","children":"The Stand Question"}]
26:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A good monitor stand adjusts height, tilt, and pivots to portrait mode. If your chosen monitor has a poor stand, budget approximately £60 for a basic monitor arm or step up to the <a href=\"/blog/monitor-arm-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">Ergotron LX</a> (~£120) for the best quality."}}]
27:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium 28:["$","$L2e",null,{"title":"Best Monitor for Home Office in 2026: Tested and Ranked","slug":"best-monitor-for-home-office"}]
29:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L2f",null,{}]}]
